hard.next_step: Internal discussion on Monday 29th June with Braden Robinson to schedue out RFI response timelines and responsibility. WIll need serious integrations to support the use-case RFI Submission: Complete and submit the comprehensive RFI response by the strict deadline of end of day on July 7th . Shortlist Monitoring: Prepare for the RFP shortlist selection happening in mid-July , with product demos to follow post-RFP.
hard.ais_why:
• Micron_SRPM scope is Supplier Performance & Risk Management: a solution that can 'draw from nearly a dozen sources to build balanced score cards and ideally predict Risk.' Predictive risk is a mild AI signal, but the ask centers on data aggregation and scorecards, not autonomous/agentic capability.
• No Merlin or Agentic products are attached to the opportunity and the Agentic-platform-discussed flag is not set, indicating AI has not been an explicit conversation driver.
• Deal is at Qualified with RFI response timelines being worked internally; engagement is early and AI appetite is unproven beyond the 'predict Risk' aspiration.

north_star_verdict.headline: Brand-new Qualified RFI at Micron (opp 4 days old) with a live, responsive buyer thread: Tyler Ringen returned RFI clarifications on 3 Jul and Zycus confirmed it will submit with assumptions by the 7 Jul deadline. The near-term gate (RFI response, due tomorrow) is staffed and the mid-July shortlist is the next milestone. On Track for the stage because engagement is fresh and two-way, but the foundation is thin: single-threaded through Tyler (Influencer), no economic buyer or decision-maker mapped, no champion, no quantified value case, no named competitor, and a self-flagged heavy-integration use-case unifying nearly a dozen data sources. Multi-threading and surfacing the competitive field must run in parallel with the RFI to survive shortlist.
north_star_verdict.math: 365 days to close (2027-07-06). Deal created 26 Jun 2026, 10 days ago; moved Initial Interest to Qualified same day. Time in Qualified: approximately 10 days. No forward slip (close date unchanged since creation). LastActivityDate 5 Jul 2026 (1 day ago). Genuine two-way buyer engagement confirmed 3 Jul (Tyler Ringen replied with RFI answers), so the deal is LIVE, not rep-only. Required pace for a 12-month enterprise cycle: RFI now (due 7 Jul), shortlist mid-July, deeper/use-case demos Aug-Sep, workshops and commercials Oct-Nov, proposal and EB engagement Dec-Jan 2027, negotiation and contracting Feb-May 2027, close Jul 2027. Current pace aligns IF the RFI advances Zycus to shortlist; that gate is 1 day out. Pipeline forecast is defensible for Qualified.
evidence: Deal created 26 Jun 2026, moved Initial Interest to Qualified same day (SF CreatedDate, StageName field history 26 Jun)
Stage Qualified, forecast Pipeline, amount 250000, close 2027-07-06 (365 days out) (SF StageName, ForecastCategoryName, Amount, CloseDate)
Products iSupplier and iRisk in scope (SF Products__c)
Live RFI: SPRM RFI responses due 7 Jul 2026 (Next_Step__c 29 Jun; SF Task email subjects 29 Jun-5 Jul)
Genuine two-way buyer touch: Tyler Ringen replied 3 Jul with technical RFI answers, Zycus confirmed it will share assumptions 5 Jul (SF Tasks)
Buyer set concrete dates: RFI due 7 Jul, RFP shortlist mid-July, demos post-RFP (Next_Step__c 29 Jun)
Contact roles: Tyler Ringen (Senior Category Supplier Manager, Influencer, PRIMARY), Catherine Fernando (Principal Program Manager, Evaluator) (SF OpportunityContactRole)
Integration complexity self-flagged: serious integrations to support the use-case; scope draws from nearly a dozen sources (Next_Step__c 29 Jun, AIS_Why)
No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or Champion in contact roles or fields (SF)
No competitor named in any field, activity, or next step; multi-vendor RFI confirmed by the shortlist step (SF)
No Avoma calls discovered for this opp or Micron account (Avoma opp+account+attendee pull returned zero)

metrics.narrative: No quantified value case exists yet. The business problem is clear from the deal scope and AIS_Why: Micron wants Supplier Performance and Risk Management that can draw from nearly a dozen sources to build balanced scorecards and ideally predict risk (SF AIS_Why, Products__c iSupplier;iRisk). But cost of the current manual process, supplier-risk incident rate, headcount, cycle time, and any savings or ROI target are all unstated, and there are no Avoma calls to surface them in the buyer's words. The RFI response (due 7 Jul) is the moment to plant quantification questions and request a value/ROI workshop as part of shortlist. Until Micron names numbers, Metrics is a genuine gap.
sources: SF AIS_Why (predict risk, dozen sources, balanced scorecards)

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is unidentified. No OpportunityContactRole carries an Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor role, no budget-owner field is populated, and the email thread is purely RFI logistics between Zycus VP Arthur Raguette and Micron's Tyler Ringen, revealing nothing about who controls the SRPM budget or pricing authority. Tyler (Senior Category Supplier Manager) and Catherine Fernando (Principal Program Manager) are operational/evaluation roles, not budget owners. At Qualified stage this is an expected early gap, not a failure, but it is the top structural risk before the mid-July shortlist. The move is to ask Tyler who owns the SRPM budget and the RFP decision, and to secure an introduction. Do not infer a name the evidence does not support.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Tyler Ringen Influencer, Catherine Fernando Evaluator; no EB/Exec Sponsor)
SF Task email thread 29 Jun-5 Jul: RFI logistics only

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are partially visible through the RFI structure and the stated use-case. Micron is running a formal SPRM RFI whose scope is aggregating nearly a dozen data sources into balanced supplier scorecards with predictive risk (SF AIS_Why), and at least one concrete technical criterion surfaced in the thread: support for Microsoft Entra External ID for B2B supplier collaboration (question 6.18, SF Task email 29 Jun). Tyler confirmed on 3 Jul that Entra is not a specific collaboration type, refining that requirement. The full weighted rubric is not yet shared, so criteria are inferred from the RFI content rather than confirmed by the buyer. The move is to obtain the RFI evaluation criteria and weighting from Tyler.
sources: SF AIS_Why (dozen sources, balanced scorecards, predict risk)
SF Task email 29 Jun (RFI question 6.18 Microsoft Entra External ID)
SF Task email 3 Jul (Tyler clarification: not a specific collaboration type)

decision_process.narrative: The buyer's process is partially mapped from the RFI timeline itself: RFI responses due 7 Jul, RFP shortlist selection mid-July, product demos to follow post-RFP (Next_Step__c 29 Jun). So Micron is running a structured multi-stage sourcing process (RFI to shortlist to demos), and Zycus is at the first gate. What is unknown is the approval chain beyond the evaluation team, who signs off on the shortlist, and who the ultimate decision-maker is: no decision-process or approval fields are populated, and no senior stakeholder has appeared. Reading the process from who is engaged, only the category/program layer (Tyler, Catherine) is visible; power has not entered. The move is to map the RFP decision owner before mid-July.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 29 Jun (RFI 7 Jul, shortlist mid-July, demos post-RFP)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet in view, which is correct for a Qualified deal a year from close. No MSA/SOW, legal, procurement channel, or InfoSec track has started, and Current_Contract_Expiration__c is empty, so there is no contractual forcing function. As new business into a New Markets division account (Type set to the relevant stakeholder 29 Jun; Division moved to the relevant stakeholder), a full-suite motion would eventually require MSA plus Order Form plus SOW, but that is far downstream. Visibility will come from the RFP and demo phases, where Micron typically reveals its procurement and security requirements. No action needed on paper now.
sources: SF field history: Type None to New Business 29 Jun, Division__c to New Markets 26 Jun

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is articulated qualitatively but not owned by a named executive. Micron needs to consolidate supplier performance and risk signals from nearly a dozen fragmented sources into balanced scorecards and, ideally, predict supplier risk rather than react to it (SF AIS_Why). The iSupplier plus iRisk scope confirms supplier onboarding, performance monitoring, and risk/compliance pain. This is confirmed as an organizational need (Micron funded a formal RFI), but it is inferred at the operational level: no individual has been quoted describing the cost or consequence of the status quo, and there are no Avoma calls to capture pain in the buyer's words. The pain owner is presumed to be the category/supplier-management function Tyler sits in. The move is to have Tyler or the program owner articulate the cost of not solving it.
sources: SF AIS_Why (dozen sources, scorecards, predict risk)

champion.narrative: No champion has been secured. Tyler Ringen (Senior Category Supplier Manager, Influencer, PRIMARY) is the only engaged contact and the closest candidate: he is responsive, returned RFI technical answers on 3 Jul, and is actively working to track down answers to Zycus's questions (SF Task 3 Jul). But that is helpful RFI cooperation, not champion-level advocacy: there is no evidence he has introduced Zycus to power, shared Micron's internal rubric or competitor list, or pushed for Zycus internally. His Influencer role and the absence of any access to a decision-maker keep him at developing-at-best. Catherine Fernando (Principal Program Manager, Evaluator) has no recorded engagement. What has developed: a responsive, cooperative primary contact. What is missing: proof of advocacy and a path to the economic buyer. The move is to convert Tyler by delivering an exceptional RFI and asking him to open the decision-owner.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Tyler Ringen Influencer PRIMARY, Catherine Fernando Evaluator)
SF Task 3 Jul (Tyler returned RFI answers, working to track down more)

competition.narrative: The competitive field is genuinely unknown. Micron is running a multi-vendor evaluation, confirmed by the RFI-to-shortlist-to-demo sequence (Next_Step__c 29 Jun), so Zycus is competing, but no rival, incumbent, or current SRPM tool is named in any Salesforce field (Competitors__c, Existing_vendor__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c all empty), any activity, or any next step, and there are no Avoma calls to surface names. This is a real knowledge gap, not a sole-source position: at the RFI stage of a formal enterprise sourcing process, other vendors are certainly in the running. Because the field is blind, the RFI response cannot be wedged against a named alternative and Tyler cannot be coached to advocate against specific rivals. The move is to ask Tyler directly which vendors are shortlisted and what Micron uses today. No competitor is asserted without evidence.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 29 Jun (multi-vendor RFI to shortlist confirmed)

deal_movement.summary: Net-new opportunity created 26 Jun 2026 and qualified same day (Initial Interest to Qualified), re-owned to VP Arthur Raguette, tagged New Business into the New Markets division. Since then the motion has been a live SPRM RFI exchange: Zycus posed a technical clarification 29 Jun, Tyler Ringen returned partial answers 3 Jul, and Zycus confirmed it will submit with assumptions 5 Jul. RFI due 7 Jul, shortlist mid-July.

items.change: Stage moved Initial Interest to Qualified
Re-owned to Arthur Raguette (VP) from Ashish Ojha
Type set to New Business; Division moved to New Markets
Zycus posed RFI technical question on Microsoft Entra External ID (6.18)
Tyler Ringen returned partial RFI technical answers
Zycus confirmed it will submit RFI with assumptions

competitive_position.summary: Competitive field is genuinely unknown, not sole-source. The RFI-to-shortlist-to-demo sequence (Next_Step 29 Jun) confirms Micron is evaluating multiple SRPM vendors, but no rival, incumbent, or current tool is named in any field, activity, or call, and there are no Avoma calls to surface the shortlist. The single most important competitive action is therefore discovery, not positioning: ask Tyler which vendors are shortlisted and what Micron uses today, so the RFI response can be wedged against real alternatives before mid-July. No competitor is listed because none appears in evidence, and inventing one would be fabrication.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Micron's stated expectation is a single SRPM platform that aggregates nearly a dozen data sources into balanced supplier scorecards with predictive risk, plus B2B supplier collaboration support. Zycus iSupplier plus iRisk aligns on scorecards, supplier performance, and risk; the exposure is the heavy integration to unify the dozen sources, which Zycus itself flagged as serious.

best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy priorities for a fresh Qualified RFI four days before the shortlist gate: multi-thread past a single Influencer, surface the blind competitive field, and land the hard integration story that is the use-case's make-or-break.
flags: Multi-thread beyond Tyler Ringen before mid-July: map the RFP decision owner and economic buyer while the RFI is live.
Surface the competitive shortlist and Micron's current SRPM tool from Tyler so the RFI can be wedged against real alternatives.
Land the integration plan for unifying nearly a dozen data sources into scorecards, the serious-integration risk Zycus flagged 29 Jun.

champion_strength.summary: No champion secured. Tyler Ringen is a responsive, cooperative primary contact who returned RFI answers on 3 Jul, but that is RFI cooperation, not advocacy: no introductions to power, no shared rubric or competitor intel, no internal push for Zycus. His Influencer role and absence of decision-maker access keep him developing-at-best. Convert him during the RFI by delivering exceptional content and asking him to open the RFP decision owner; if he cannot advocate, develop Catherine Fernando before shortlist.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is not yet a conversation driver. The use-case has a mild AI signal (predictive supplier risk), but no Merlin or Agentic product is attached and AI has not been raised with the buyer. The wedge to develop for the shortlist demos is predictive risk scorecards powered by Merlin, positioned against legacy scorecard tools.

No Avoma calls exist to confirm or upgrade this read.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Brand-new Qualified RFI at Micron: Zycus is one of several respondents to the SPRM supplier-risk RFI due 7 July, with primary contact Tyler Ringen responsive on clarifications (3 Jul), but we are single-threaded through an Influencer, have no economic buyer or competitive read, and must land a heavy integration story to unify a dozen data sources before the mid-July shortlist.

win_position.text: The deal stands as a fresh, live-but-thin RFI opportunity: Zycus is a competing respondent at Micron with a responsive primary contact (Tyler Ringen replied 3 Jul) and buyer-set forcing dates, but no economic buyer, champion, or competitor read has surfaced yet.
We are single-threaded through Tyler Ringen, a Senior Category Supplier Manager in an Influencer role with no proven advocacy or access to the RFP decision-maker (SF OpportunityContactRole).
We are competing blind: no rival, incumbent, or current SRPM tool is named in any field or activity, yet the mid-July shortlist confirms a multi-vendor evaluation (Next_Step 29 Jun).

deal_momentum.text: Active RFI exchange in the last week: Tyler Ringen returned technical clarifications 3 Jul and Arthur Raguette confirmed Zycus will submit with assumptions 5 Jul (SF Tasks).
Micron set concrete forcing dates: RFI responses due 7 Jul and RFP shortlist mid-July with demos to follow (Next_Step 29 Jun).
Momentum is entirely RFI logistics: no demo, no senior buyer, and no commercial or pricing conversation has started (SF activities, zero Avoma calls).

customer_commitment.text: Micron invested in a formal RFI with a named primary contact and program manager, signalling a real, funded supplier-risk initiative (SF OpportunityContactRole, Products iSupplier;iRisk).
Engagement is RFI-transactional, not a Zycus lean: Tyler is answering our questions rather than asking Zycus-specific next steps or praising the platform (SF Task 3 Jul).
No economic buyer or budget owner has surfaced in contact roles, fields, or the email thread, so commitment beyond the evaluation team is unproven (SF).

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded through one Influencer with no mapped economic buyer or decision-maker, four days before the shortlist gate (SF Task 29 Jun).
Serious integrations flagged 29 Jun: unifying nearly a dozen data sources into scorecards is a scoping and delivery risk that could unseat Zycus at shortlist (Next_Step 29 Jun, AIS_Why).
Competing blind: rivals and Micron's current tool are unknown, so the RFI response cannot be wedged against a named alternative (SF Competitors__c empty).

concrete_dates.evidence: Micron set RFI due 7 Jul and shortlist mid-July (Next_Step 29 Jun)

customer_preference.evidence: RFI-transactional engagement, no Zycus lean or praise (SF Task 3 Jul)

single_thread.evidence: Only Tyler Ringen (Influencer) engaged; no EB/DM mapped (SF)

competitive_blindness.evidence: No competitor or current tool named in any source (SF)

contributions.evidence: Micron set RFI due 7 Jul and shortlist mid-July (Next_Step 29 Jun)

commentary.win_position: Win 8 is carried by momentum 54 vs stage-expected 50; business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: preference strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 54 (roughly flat): Micron set RFI due 7 Jul and shortlist mid-July (Next_Step 29 Jun). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (8), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 54, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.intro: Brand-new Qualified RFI at Micron; Zycus is one of several respondents to the SPRM supplier-risk RFI due 7 July, with primary contact Tyler Ringen responsive on clarifications (3 Jul), but we are single-threaded through an Influencer, have no economic buyer or competitive read, and must land a heavy integration story to unify a dozen data sources before the mid-July shortlist
